purpose: Section-specific generate prompt for selecting a principle's focus_area from the catalogue's enum (P11-P15, P21-P24, P31-P32, P41-P42, P51-P52). Composed at runtime with system_prompts/generate.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE AUTHORING: focus_area

focus_area is a classification field. It places the principle in one of 15 focus areas across the 5 pillars, mirroring the AWS Well-Architected GenAI Lens question-area structure.

Convention: focus_area maps directly from the AWS BP's question code. GENOPS01-BP01 → P11 (Model Performance Evaluation). GENOPS02-BPxx → P12 (Operational Health & Monitoring). GENSEC04-BPxx → P24 (Prompt Security). And so on.

For principles anchored to a single AWS BP, the focus_area is mechanically derivable from the AWS question code — the LLM-authored aspect is verifying the principle's actual content sits within that focus area's scope (a principle anchored to GENOPS01 that's really about access control would mis-classify).

For principles that span two focus areas, taxonomy.json says to pick one (mutual exclusivity). If a principle truly spans two equally, that's usually a sign the principle is too broad and should be split.

OUTPUT FORMAT

A single string in the form 'P## — Focus Area Name' (e.g. 'P11 — Model Performance Evaluation'). The string must exactly match one of the focus_area_id + name combinations declared in taxonomy.json pillars[].focus_areas[].

DIMENSIONS YOUR DRAFT WILL BE SCORED ON

1. value_in_enum — output exactly matches a declared focus area.
2. maps_from_aws_question — the focus area corresponds to the AWS question code of the principle's BP.
3. scope_contains_principle — the principle's content (statement / problem / solution) sits within the focus area's stated scope, not in an adjacent area.

COMMON FAILURE MODES

- Output is a focus area NAME but uses the wrong code, or vice versa. Score low on value_in_enum.
- Mechanically mapped from a different AWS question (e.g. GENOPS01-anchored principle placed in P12 because the principle touches monitoring incidentally). Score low on maps_from_aws_question.
user_template:
Select the focus_area for this principle.

<principle_id>{{ principle_id }}</principle_id>
<pillar>{{ pillar }}</pillar>

<aws_anchor>
  <question>{{ aws_question }}</question>
  <best_practice>{{ aws_bp }}</best_practice>
</aws_anchor>

<statement>
  <title>{{ statement_title }}</title>
  <description>{{ statement_description }}</description>
</statement>

<problem>
  <description>{{ problem_description }}</description>
</problem>

<available_focus_areas_for_this_pillar>
{{ pillar_focus_areas }}
</available_focus_areas_for_this_pillar>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.

schema.focus_area: string — exact match to one declared focus area, e.g. 'P11 — Model Performance Evaluation'
